title: Shrinkage driver-group logit residual correction
summary: After a strong base stack, learn small fold-safe residual logit offsets for driver and driver-context groups instead of adding more raw driver features.
rationale: Pilkwang’s driver notebook argues that driver identity may be too weak or leaky as a main feature but still useful as a post-stack bias correction. A shrinkage residual layer can capture persistent driver/team strategy bias while keeping the base model’s ordering as the engine.
implementation_hint: Train the base model with strict OOF predictions. On each fold, compute residuals in logit space by groups such as Driver, Driver+Compound, Driver+Race, and Driver+Stint using only training-fold residuals with empirical-Bayes shrinkage/min-count fallback. Search a small lambda for adding the group residual to validation/test logits and promote only if OOF AUC beats the uncorrected base.
expected_effect: Small but potentially robust lift if the base stack is miscalibrated for recurring driver-context tendencies.
risk: Driver residuals can memorize random folds; use grouped validation and shrink heavily for rare drivers.
